summary: The Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals, and Kevin Maloff to discuss potential amendments to the Zoning Resolution. Key topics included extending panhandle/flag lot provisions to R2 and R3 districts, establishing rear yard setbacks for PUD residential lots, revising accessory dwelling unit (ADU) size and construction requirements, and creating yard sale regulations to address nuisance properties.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
action_items: Director Langenheim to prepare illustrations and diagrams of flag/panhandle lot scenarios including recent variance examples for the next workshop
Director Langenheim to draft language for 35-foot rear yard setback (70-foot facade-to-facade) for PUD residential lots
Director Langenheim to draft ADU language with 500 sq ft minimum, 1,000 sq ft maximum, 50% of primary structure cap, limited to R1 and R2 zones, attached to primary residence
Director Langenheim to research whether other townships have language allowing living quarters within detached garages/pole barns
Director Langenheim to research pitfalls and pros/cons of proposed ADU changes
Director Langenheim to check with Clermont County on septic system requirements for ADUs
Director Langenheim to draft yard sale regulations including 72-hour display limits, six sales per year maximum, and complaint-driven enforcement
Staff to consult legal counsel regarding yard sale/transient vendor enforcement at trailer parks
key_amounts: 25-foot minimum road frontage for panhandle/flag lots
500-foot maximum panhandle stem length from roadway
10,800 square feet minimum PUD lot size
35-foot rear yard setback proposed for PUD lots (70 feet facade-to-facade)
500 square foot proposed minimum ADU size
1,000 square foot proposed maximum ADU size
50% of primary dwelling proposed ADU size cap
1,400 square foot minimum for single-family homes
$200 per square foot estimated new construction cost
72 hours proposed maximum yard sale display duration
6 yard sales per year proposed maximum
notable_topics: Extending panhandle/flag lot provisions from R1 to R2 and R3 zoning districts
Establishing rear yard setbacks for PUD residential lots (currently no hard minimum exists)
Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) size requirements and whether to allow detached new construction
Yard sale regulation to address nuisance properties using perpetual yard sales to store junk
Concerns about PUD development density and developer flexibility vs. community character
Senior housing and aging-in-place considerations for ADU policy
Septic system capacity limitations for ADUs on lots without sewer
Traffic safety concerns near Lakeshore area weekend vendor setups
Unintended consequences of ADUs on future property resale
attendees: Steve Pilgrim (Zoning Commission, 6336 Telford Farm Lane)
notes: This was a workshop/discussion session with no formal zoning amendments voted on; all topics were discussed for future guidance and drafting. Kevin Maloff emphasized making development processes intentionally rigorous to protect community character. Historical reference was made to a former mobile home/mother-in-law suite rule that has since been removed from the code. The transcript was truncated before the meeting fully concluded.
title: BOT Special Meeting Zoning Workshop 3-31-26
